notes: Covers session lifecycle, Telegram routing, compact flow, and AI provider. See .rune/INVARIANTS.md for the prose rules (INV-1..INV-12). logic-guardian MUST block edits here without explicit statement of what is preserved.

functions.name: startSession
functions.signature: (opts: StartSessionOpts) => Promise<{ sessionId: string; shortId: string }>
functions.description: Creates in-memory session + DB record + launches CLI/SDK. Resume path clears old cliSessionId before creating new session.

functions.name: killSession
functions.description: Terminates session: clears idle/cleanup timers, deletes session settings, kills SDK/CLI process, captures shortId, updates status to 'ended', calls endSessionRecord. Single entry point for all kill paths.

functions.name: endSessionRecord
functions.description: Marks session ended in DB, clears shortId + cliSessionId. CANONICAL end path — every kill must go through this.

functions.name: createSessionRecord
functions.description: Inserts new session row, generates shortId (collision-free), returns shortId.

functions.name: clearCliSessionId
functions.description: Clears cliSessionId on old session when resuming. Prevents double-attach.

functions.name: getSessionConfig
functions.description: Returns cached SessionConfig. On cache miss, loads persisted idleTimeoutMs from telegramSessionMappings. idleTimeoutEnabled=false → 0 (never kill).

functions.name: resetIdleTimer
functions.signature: (sessionId: string, chatId: number, topicId?: number) => void
functions.description: Bumps generation counter, schedules warning (5m before kill) + kill timer. Stale generations bail via guard.

functions.name: setIdleTimeout
functions.description: Updates cfg + persists to DB. Writer entry point; all external writes go here.

functions.name: routeSessionMessage
functions.signature: (bridge, chatId, topicId, sessionId, msg: BrowserIncomingMessage) => Promise<void>
functions.description: Exhaustive switch over BrowserIncomingMessage.type. Adding a new variant requires a case here. Cases: assistant, stream_event, stream_event_batch, result, permission_request, session_init, context_breakdown, context_update, cost_warning, cli_disconnected, status_change (ended/compacting/idle), compact_handoff (summarizing/compacting/done), tool_progress, user_message.

functions.name: handleContextUpdate
functions.signature: (bridge, chatId, topicId, sessionId, contextUsedPercent: number) => Promise<void>
functions.description: Sends Telegram warning when context reaches session.state.compact_threshold (user-configurable, default 75%). Gated by compactWarningSent set (cleared post-compact via status_change=idle).

functions.name: handleResultMessage
functions.signature: (bridge, chatId, topicId, sessionId, result: CLIResultMessage) => Promise<void>
functions.description: Resets idle timer + sends inline token bar after each turn.

functions.name: sendSessionSummary
functions.description: Polls for summarizer result up to 15s, renders summary with files/metrics.

functions.name: checkSmartCompact
functions.description: Evaluates session.state.compact_mode (manual/smart/aggressive) against compact_threshold. Aggressive triggers immediately; smart waits for idle then handoff.

functions.name: triggerSmartCompactHandoff
functions.description: Broadcasts compact_handoff { stage: 'summarizing' } → sends handoff prompt to CLI → schedulePostHandoffCompact.

functions.name: resolveProvider
functions.description: Priority chain: DB ai.* settings → env vars → Anthropic fallback. When ai.baseUrl is set, routes ALL Claude-API calls through OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

functions.name: callOpenAICompatible
functions.description: 3-attempt retry with exponential backoff. Each attempt 1-15s. BAD provider config = 15-45s delay per AI call.

functions.name: startIdleTimer
functions.description: Per-session idle timeout in WS/web path. DIFFERENT from telegram-idle-manager — edits to one path MUST check the other.

co_change_groups.name: session-end-flow
co_change_groups.reason: All three paths must clear shortId + sessions.cliSessionId + telegramSessionMappings.cliSessionId consistently. Changing one table's write = change the others.

co_change_groups.name: compact-flow
co_change_groups.reason: compact_handoff broadcast → router switch → event handler must all be kept in sync. Threshold reads from session.state, never hardcoded.

co_change_groups.name: dual-path-idle
co_change_groups.reason: Two independent idle-kill implementations (web vs telegram). Fix in one = check the other for regression (INV-10).

co_change_groups.name: ai-provider-lifecycle
co_change_groups.reason: Provider resolution chain affects ALL internal AI calls. Adding ai.* settings requires: (1) load in resolveProvider, (2) add to settings-tab-ai.tsx UI, (3) add to handleDisable keys array (INV-12).

co_change_groups.name: message-routing-exhaustiveness
co_change_groups.reason: Every new BrowserIncomingMessage variant (shared/types/session.ts) needs a case here — otherwise telegram users silently lose visibility into that event type (INV-7, INV-8).
